raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "exhortation urging the soul to practice self-criticism and humility in view of impending mortality",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "life is finite and the opportunity for self-respect will soon end",
    "temporary self-abuse or contempt is a worthwhile or necessary exercise",
    "the addressee (soul) possesses the agency to direct such attitudes toward itself",
    "self-respect is time-bound and not available after death"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "coherent within a Stoic framework linking memento mori to disciplined self-examination; the imperative follows logically from the premise of limited time",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "reader accepts mortality as motivation for honest self-assessment and values balanced self-critique over unchecked self-esteem",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "taken as endorsement of destructive self-hatred, or if the 'end of time' premise is used to induce despair rather than urgency for constructive reflection"
}
